parsed_response.routing_rationale: CommercialExpert needed for delay exposure quantification (15k/day) and accelerated logistics cost (48k); RiskExpert required to resolve conflicting onsite assertions between progress report and supplier/logistics records; ScheduleExpert required to assess float, forecast slippage (13 days), and baseline deviation (21 days). All three experts are evidence-supported and within executable scope.

request.system_instructions: You are a Project Recovery Council live experiment agent. Return one JSON object only.

claims.milestone_slip_forecast: MS-COMMISSIONING-READY is forecast to slip by 13 days (SCH-DELIVERY-001), directly driven by the 21-day delivery delay and insufficient float (8 days) to absorb the shift.
claims.onsite_status_conflict: Conflicting claims exist: progress report (PRG-ONSITE-001) asserts EQ-GEN-SKID-01 is onsite and released; supplier correspondence (SUP-NOT-ARRIVED-001) and logistics status (LOG-STATUS-001) confirm it remains in transit and has not arrived.
claims.recovery_approval_risk: Recovery option REC-ACCEL-LOGISTICS cannot be authorized without resolving the onsite conflict, as misalignment invalidates installation readiness assumptions and jeopardizes approval of time-critical interventions.

warnings: Human confirmation required to resolve contradictory onsite status before approving any recovery action — per RISK-001 mitigation directive.
Unresolved conflict invalidates progress-based schedule logic (e.g., installation start assumptions); no automated reconciliation is permitted per protocol.

warnings: ScheduleExpert.installation_total_float_consumed_days (13) contradicts SCH-DELIVERY-001: installation_total_float_days = 8; consumption cannot exceed available float.
ScheduleExpert.installation_total_float_remaining_days (-5) contradicts SCH-DELIVERY-001: remaining float must be non-negative; observed value violates schedule semantics.

RiskExpert.milestone_slip_forecast is unsupported because it repeats a quantitative schedule claim already validated by ScheduleExpert and falls outside RiskExpert's permitted claim set per role policy.
